_case: F-18a relocated (plan §3): the drill proc row's liveness used to be written straight onto the registry row by _reconcile_drill_rows — a second, competing classifier. It is now merged as tier-2 EVIDENCE (`proc_liveness`) and the single classifier weighs it: a live correlated process outranks the registry row's tier-3 'no transcript → dead/queued' derivation. Same outcome, one decision point.

cases._case: registry row would derive `queued` from mtime, but a live proc is correlated → working (tier 2)
no correlated proc → the tier-3 derivation stands
proc evidence never DOWNGRADES a working registry row (guard: absorb is one-way)
